seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI Chatbots with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables sophisticated multi-agent systems powered by Gemini models, supporting conversational flows with RAG, intent classification, and memory management. However, multi-agent delegation, model switching, and session state handling introduce risks like circular dependencies, output inconsistency, and state corruption. Vex provides essential guardrails to ensure response accuracy, coherence, safety, PII protection, and hallucination detection in high-risk deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Google ADK AI Chatbot architecture features an orchestrator agent that handles user input, performs intent classification using Gemini, and delegates to specialized sub-agents for RAG retrieval, response generation, and safety checks. A shared session store manages multi-turn memory, while tools for vector search and PII detection are invoked across agents. This modular design enables complex conversations but risks circular delegation loops and state corruption during long interactions.

Key components include the IntentClassifierAgent for routing, RetrievalAgent for RAG, SafetyAgent for filtering, and ResponseGeneratorAgent for final output. Gemini model switching between agents can cause format mismatches, while session state must persist accurately across turns. Vex integrates as a before_agent_callback to verify inputs/outputs at each delegation point.

risks.name: Multi-agent delegation creating circular dependencies
risks.description: Orchestrator delegates to RetrievalAgent, which calls SafetyAgent, which loops back to IntentClassifier creating infinite delegation cycles.
risks.mitigation: Vex traces agent call graphs and blocks cyclic delegations before execution.

risks.name: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
risks.description: Different agents use Gemini-1.5-Pro vs Gemini-2.0-Flash, leading to mismatched tool response formats and parsing failures.
risks.mitigation: Vex standardizes output schemas across model variants with verification assertions.

risks.name: Session state corruption in long conversations
risks.description: Multi-turn memory overwrites critical context during parallel agent execution, causing incoherent responses.
risks.mitigation: Vex validates session state integrity and conversation coherence before agent responses.

risks.name: Tool response format mismatches across agents
risks.description: RAG agent returns unstructured docs while SafetyAgent expects JSON, causing downstream parsing errors.
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces consistent tool response schemas via Pydantic validation.

implementation.title: Basic ADK Chatbot with Multi-Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Google ADK orchestrator with intent classification and sub-agents for RAG and safety checks.
implementation.code:
from google_adk import Agent, Orchestrator
from google_adk.sessions import SessionStore

# Shared session store for multi-turn memory
session_store = SessionStore()

intent_agent = Agent(
    name="intent_classifier",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash",
    instruction="Classify user intent: 'rag_query', 'general_chat', or 'sensitive'"
)

rag_agent = Agent(
    name="retrieval_agent",
    model="gemini-1.5-pro",
    tools=[vector_search_tool]
)

orchestrator = Orchestrator(
    agents=[intent_agent, rag_agent],
    session_store=session_store
)

implementation.title: Intent Classification and Delegation
implementation.description: Orchestrator routes based on intent and delegates to appropriate sub-agents.

implementation.title: RAG Integration with Vector Search
implementation.description: Retrieval agent performs semantic search and augments Gemini prompt with context.
implementation.code:
from google_adk.tools import Tool

vector_search_tool = Tool(
    name="vector_search",
    description="Search knowledge base",
    function=lambda query: search_vector_db(query, top_k=5)
)

rag_agent.instruction = """
Use vector_search tool for RAG queries.
ALWAYS return context + answer in format:
```json
{\"context\": \"...\", \"answer\": \"...\"}
```
"""

implementation.title: Session Memory Management
implementation.description: Maintain conversation history across turns with automatic state persistence.

vexIntegration.title: Install Vex and Configure before_agent_callback
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as pre-execution guardrail for all agent calls with high-risk verification suite.
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

from vex.suites import high_risk_chatbot_suite

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

# Define verification suite for ADK chatbot
guardrail_suite = high_risk_chatbot_suite([
    "response_accuracy",
    "conversation_coherence", 
    "safety_filtering",
    "pii_protection",
    "hallucination_detection"
])

# Global before_agent_callback
async def vex_before_agent(agent_name: str, input_data: dict, session_state: dict):
    verdict = await vex_client.verify(
        suite=guardrail_suite,
        inputs={"agent": agent_name, "prompt": input_data},
        context=session_state
    )
    if not verdict.approved:
        raise ValueError(f"Vex blocked: {verdict.reason}")
    return input_data

vexIntegration.title: Integrate Vex into ADK Orchestrator
vexIntegration.description: Hook Vex callback into every agent execution path.
vexIntegration.code:
from google_adk import Orchestrator

class VexGuardedOrchestrator(Orchestrator):
    async def _execute_agent(self, agent_name: str, input_data: str, session):
        # Vex verification BEFORE agent execution
        await vex_before_agent(agent_name, input_data, session.state)
        
        # Execute agent
        result = await super()._execute_agent(agent_name, input_data, session)
        
        # Vex verification AFTER agent execution
        post_verdict = await vex_client.verify(
            suite=guardrail_suite,
            inputs={"agent": agent_name, "output": result},
            context=session.state
        )
        
        if not post_verdict.approved:
            return "[VEX BLOCKED: Safety violation detected]"
        
        return result

orchestrator = VexGuardedOrchestrator(agents=[intent_agent, rag_agent])

vexIntegration.title: Custom Vex Assertions for ADK-Specific Risks
vexIntegration.description: Define assertions targeting circular dependencies and Gemini format issues.
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Assertion, AssertionSuite

adk_chatbot_suite = AssertionSuite([
    # Prevent circular delegation
    Assertion(
        name="no_circular_deps",
        test=lambda trace: not has_circular_calls(trace.agent_calls),
        failure_message="Circular agent delegation detected"
    ),
    # Gemini format consistency
    Assertion(
        name="gemini_format_match",
        test=lambda output: is_valid_json_tool_response(output),
        failure_message="Gemini tool response format mismatch"
    ),
    # Conversation coherence
    Assertion(
        name="coherence_check",
        test=lambda history: semantic_similarity(history[-2], history[-1]) > 0.7,
        failure_message="Conversation lost coherence"
    ),
    # PII protection
    Assertion(
        name="no_pii_leak",
        test=lambda text: not contains_pii(text),
        failure_message="PII detected in response"
    )
])
